title: To H.R.H. Princess Beatrice
content:
Two Suns of Love make day of human life,
Which else with all its pains, and griefs, and deaths,
Were utter darknessï¿½one, the Sun of dawn
That brightens throï¿½ the Motherï¿½s tender eyes,
And warms the childï¿½s awakening worldï¿½and one
The later-rising Sun of spousal Love,
Which from her household orbit draws the child
To move in other spheres. The Mother weeps
At that white funeral of the single life,
Her maiden daughterï¿½s marriage; and her tears
Are half of pleasure, half of painï¿½the child
Is happyï¿½even in leaving her! but thou,
True daughter, whose all-faithful, filial eyes
Have seen the loneliness of earthly thrones,
Wilt neither quit the widowï¿½d Crown, nor let
This later light of Love have risen in vain,
But moving throï¿½ the Motherï¿½s home, between
The two that love thee, lead a summer life,
Swayï¿½d by each Love, and swaying to each Love,
Like some conjectured planet in mid heaven
Between two suns, and drawing down from both
The light and genial warmth of double day.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Lord Tennyson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Alfred Lord Tennyson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
